ogTitle: Main Arm vs Main Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Main Arm and Main Creek.
heading: Main Arm vs Main Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Main Arm, New South Wales and Main Creek, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Main Arm has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Main Creek (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Main Arm or Main Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Main Arm has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Main Creek scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Main Arm scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Main Creek scores 34/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Main Arm or Main Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Main Arm has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
